recalls.Description: Umarex USA is recalling the T4E TR50 Air Pistol, a CO2-powered device designed for recreational and training purposes. This air pistol, which measures approximately nine inches in length, can hold six .50 caliber rounds and is constructed from black plastic polymer. The affected models include 2292112, 2280182, and 2280199, identifiable by the T4E logo and 'TR50' engraving on the left side of the muzzle, with the serial number located on the right side.
recalls.Title: Umarex USA Issues Recall for T4E TR50 Air Pistols Due to Potential Impact Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the affected air pistols and reach out to Umarex at 877-212-1036, available from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, they can email service@umarex.com or visit http://t4eguns.com/recall for further details.

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 30 reports of the CO2 cartridge unintentionally ejecting with force, including seven reports of injury, such as fractured hands, broken fingers and contusions.

Retailers.Name: Available at Big 5 stores in the Western U.S., Modern Combat Sports in Vista, CA, and online at https://mcsus.com, Amazon.com, and Valken.com from May 2022 to July 2023, priced around $110.

Importers.Name: Umarex USA, of Fort Smith, Arkansas

Hazards.Name: Risk of CO2 cartridge being forcefully ejected from the handle

Remedies.Name: Register for a complimentary replacement air pistol and receive free return shipping for the recalled item

recalls.metaDescription: Umarex USA recalls T4E TR50 Air Pistols due to impact hazards. Stop using and contact for a free replacement.

recalls.Description: Crosman is recalling its Icon .177 and .22 Caliber Air Rifles, which are PCP-powered bolt-action models identified by the numbers CPI77S and CPI22S. These model numbers can be found on the left side of the rifle, while the model name is displayed on the right side. The recall is due to a potential safety issue where an uncocked and loaded air rifle may discharge unexpectedly if jolted or dropped, leading to possible injury.
recalls.Title: Crosman Recalls Icon Air Rifles Due to Risk of Injury
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Crosman at 800-724-7486 from 9 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. ET, Monday to Friday. Additional information is available at www.crosman.com/iconrecall or by clicking on the "Recall" link on the Crosman homepage. You can also email iconrecall@crosman.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Crosman Icon .177 Caliber Air Rifle (CPI77S)
Recalled Crosman Icon .22 Caliber Air Rifle (CPI22S)
Recalled Crosman Model Number location on air rifle
The name of the model "icon" on the right-hand side of the rifle

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of an unintended discharge. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Do It Best Hardware, True Value, Service Star, and Sportsman's Guide stores nationwide, as well as online at www.Crosman.com, www.pyramydair.com, and www.Amazon.com from March 2022 through March 2024, priced around $310.

Importers.Name: Crosman Corp., of Bloomfield, New York

Hazards.Name: Risk of unexpected discharge leading to injury

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the recalled air rifles immediately and contact Crosman for a refund via store credit or check after returning the product. Crosman will provide a shipping box with instructions and prepaid postage to those who register at www.crosman.com/iconrecall. The company is also reaching out to all known purchasers.

recalls.metaDescription: Crosman recalls Icon air rifles due to injury risks. Stop using immediately and contact for refunds. Learn more about the recall.

recalls.Description: Ravin Crossbows has issued a recall for eight models of the R500 series crossbows due to a safety concern. These crossbows measure approximately 8 inches wide when un-cocked and about 28 inches in length. Each model features a split limb design with cams positioned between the upper and lower limbs, and they are available in slate gray and camo finishes. The recalled crossbows come equipped with a scope, quiver, cocking handle, quiver bracket, and six arrows. The Ravin logo and model names are displayed on a decal on both upper limbs, and the serial number can be found on the left side of the rail above the trigger.
recalls.Title: Ravin R500 Series Crossbows Recalled Due to Potential Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Ravin Crossbows at 888-298-6335 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, email R500recall@ravincrossbows.com, or visit https://www.ravincrossbows.com/r500recall. More information is also available on their website under "Safety & Instruction" and then "Recall Notices."

Products.Name: Ravin R500 Crossbow
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 13,300 (In addition, about 425 were sold in Canada)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of the crossbow unexpectedly discharging. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Ravin Crossbows LLC, of Superior, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Unexpected discharge when nearly cocked, leading to potential injury.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled crossbows and contact Ravin Crossbows for a free repair.

recalls.metaDescription: Ravin Crossbows recalls R500 series models due to injury risks. Contact them for a free repair and stop using the affected crossbows.
